purpose: Decide how to approach a technology-independent conceptual enterprise data model.
ask_if_missing: whether the user wants conceptual model or physical design
which business domain is being modeled
whether current forms/screens are authoritative or only clues
whether names, identifiers, and characteristics require history

branches.use_when: The task is about stable business meaning, shared language, or cross-system integration.
branches.recommend: Build an architect-view conceptual model before choosing database/service structures.

branches.use_when: The task centers on current forms, reports, screens, or legacy tables.
branches.recommend: Extract business facts from artifacts, then decide which artifacts are information resources versus implementation details.

branches.use_when: The task has many variable attributes by entity type or source.
branches.recommend: Use characteristic/value patterns with legal values, units, sources, and effective dates.

branches.use_when: The task includes customers, employees, vendors, owners, or managers.
branches.recommend: Model parties and roles/relationships rather than multiplying person-like entity classes.

branches.use_when: The task includes cataloged products, services, procedures, materials, or equipment.
branches.recommend: Separate instances from types and specifications before modeling structure and characteristics.

failure_modes: Modeling a current technology artifact as a fundamental business entity.
Skipping abstraction-level choice and mixing concept, logical, and physical concerns.
Using columns for every possible variable descriptor.
Conflating a party with a role the party plays.
Treating a report or document as canonical when it only queries other facts.
output_checks: Separate business concepts from technology artifacts.
Identify party, geography, asset, activity, time, information-resource, and accounting families when relevant.
List business rules not enforceable by structure alone.
